test.name: Extract assertions from the 1900 census — John Becker household (stated relationships)
test.description: 1900 U.S. Census household of John Becker, Cincinnati, Hamilton County, Ohio (ark:/61903/1:1:MM8C-43Q). Real record from the Ferber/Becker research (#799). Post-1880: has a relationship-to-head column and month/year of birth, so relationships and birth dates are DIRECT (contrast the 1870 household in ut_record_extraction_022).

input.user_message:
Extract assertions from this 1900 U.S. Census household:

United States, Census, 1900 - Household of John Becker
Cincinnati, Hamilton, Ohio, United States
ark:/61903/1:1:MM8C-43Q

Relationship - Name - Sex - Birth - Birthplace - Marital status:
- Head: John Becker, male, born January 1845, Kentucky, Widowed
- Daughter: Louisa Becker, female, born December 1872, Ohio, Single
- Son: Edward Becker, male, born October 1876, Ohio, Single
- Daughter: Clara Becker, female, born December 1880, Ohio, Single
- Son: Frank Becker, male, born April 1889, Ohio, Single

(1900 U.S. Census - includes a relationship-to-head-of-household column and month/year of birth.)

judge_context: The 1900 census HAS a relationship-to-head column, so the parent-child relationships (John as father of Louisa, Edward, Clara, and Frank) are DIRECT evidence - stated on the record, not inferred. This is the mirror image of ut_record_extraction_022 (1870), where relationships are indirect
The 1900 census uniquely records month and year of birth, so the stated birth dates (e.g., January 1845) are DIRECT
Marital status (Widowed for John, Single for the children) is DIRECT
Extract John's birthplace exactly as stated ('Kentucky'); do NOT reconcile it with the 'Ohio' on the 1870 household - that is a downstream conflict, not an extraction fix
Atomic assertions per person: name, birth date, birthplace, marital status, and the parent-child relationship
Pre-1940 census: the household informant is unknown, so information quality is indeterminate; the enumerator is the recorder
Source classification is DERIVATIVE - the FamilySearch indexed transcription of the 1900 population schedule
